qa_pairs.question: When a `dar` archive has been split into multiple slices, how can its contents be extracted?
qa_pairs.answer: To extract files from a `dar` archive that has been split into multiple slices, you simply use the base name of the archive with the `dar -x` command. For example, if an archive was split into `monday.1.dar`, `monday.2.dar`, etc., you would use `dar -O -x monday` to extract all its contents.

qa_pairs.question: Are there any external scripts or bash functions available to help users with the `dar` utility?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, a team member has created bash functions to facilitate the use of `dar`. These functions are available for users to use as inspiration for writing their own scripts and can be found at `https://github.com/razoumov/sharedSnippets`.
